jokes.text: Whatever. It doesn't really matter. Doesn't matter? Every decision we make opens up a universe of possibilities. I put it to you that, as sentient beings, each choice we make is precious.
jokes.setup: Fry dismisses his choice as not mattering
jokes.punchline: Leela launches into overwrought philosophical speech about the importance of every decision
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Fry's casualness and Leela's philosophical intensity, sets up episode theme

jokes.text: Well, in that case, don't wear them. Ah, who asked you?
jokes.setup: After Leela's speech about precious decisions
jokes.punchline: She immediately makes his decision for him, then he rejects her advice
jokes.explanation: Nice payoff to previous setup showing hypocrisy, economical execution

jokes.text: Oh! I like your nerd glasses. Are you a college student? A college student? Because if you are, I'd like to invite you to a hot sorority party tonight.
jokes.setup: Amy sees Fry with glasses and assumes he's a college student
jokes.punchline: Immediate invitation to sorority party based solely on glasses
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Amy's shallowness, absurd logic about glasses = college student

jokes.text: Yes, I'd like to enroll in college. You're still talking to me. Oops.
jokes.setup: Fry eagerly agrees to enroll in college for the party
jokes.punchline: Amy realizes she's still engaging with him and awkwardly exits
jokes.explanation: Nice cringe moment showing both characters' social awkwardness

jokes.text: $10,000 tuition? I can't afford that! Psst. Over there. Where? I mean over here. Sorry, I forgot where I was.
jokes.setup: Fry can't afford tuition, mysterious voice offers help
jokes.punchline: Robot gets confused about his own location while trying to be sneaky
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy undermining the dramatic loan shark introduction

jokes.text: Buongiorno, Bender. We, the robot mafia, are prepared to lend you $10,000 at an interest rate of 10,000%. That's easy to remember. I'll take it!
jokes.setup: Robot mafia offers predatory loan with absurd interest rate
jokes.punchline: Bender focuses on how easy it is to remember rather than the obvious trap
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - missing the important detail for trivial convenience

jokes.text: Yo, hombre, you think you're cool enough to sit way in back with us college toughs? Yes, I do. Yo, in that case you're all right, man. You want to join a gang? I'm gonna go with yes again.
jokes.setup: Gang member tests if Bender is cool enough for back row
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to gang membership, Bender casually agrees
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from seating arrangement to gang membership, typical Bender impulsiveness

jokes.text: Mr. Bender, I realize class has only been in session for 32 seconds, but you've fallen in with a bad crowd. I know, right? Also, I've decided to drop out of school.
jokes.setup: Professor notes Bender has fallen in with bad crowd in record time
jokes.punchline: Bender agrees and immediately decides to drop out entirely
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and timing, perfect Bender character moment with great economy

jokes.text: Well, I got my tuition back, minus $9,000 for the damage I caused to the dean's wife.
jokes.setup: Bender mentions getting his tuition back
jokes.punchline: Reveals he caused $9,000 damage to the dean's wife (implying violence)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually, perfectly in character for Bender's destructive nature

jokes.text: Hey, what's that? Is it drugs? Nah, it ain't drugs. But it's a lot like drugs. It's called spark.
jokes.setup: Bender asks if substance is drugs
jokes.punchline: It's not drugs but is exactly like drugs, called 'spark' (robot drug pun)
jokes.explanation: Clever robot-specific drug name with good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: You want a hit? Never heard of it. Sure.
jokes.setup: Offered drugs he's never heard of
jokes.punchline: Immediately agrees despite complete ignorance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender impulsiveness, good economy in delivery

jokes.text: You like it? I can take it or leave it. I guess I'll take it.
jokes.setup: Asked if he likes the drug
jokes.punchline: Claims indifference then immediately chooses to take more
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction showing instant addiction despite claimed indifference

jokes.text: It's a big decision, G. Are you ready to get a gang tattoo that will mark you as a stone cold thug for life? I'm ready. I'll take that big purple dinosaur, please. On the face.
jokes.setup: Serious moment about getting intimidating gang tattoo for life
jokes.punchline: Chooses cute purple dinosaur on his face
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of tough gang expectations with childish choice, highly quotable

jokes.text: Oh, snap! It's our rival gang! We have a rival gang? I hate them!
jokes.setup: Rival gang appears, creating tension
jokes.punchline: Bender learns they have rivals and instantly hates them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender loyalty switch, instantly adopting gang mentality

jokes.text: Whew! That was close, eh, Fabricio?
jokes.setup: After gang shootout, Bender turns to his friend
jokes.punchline: Implied visual of Fabricio being dead/severely injured
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy beat showing consequences, Bender's obliviousness to friend's fate

jokes.text: Hello, handsome. Might I procure your services? Uh, what do I have to do? Oh, nothing sordid, I assure you. Simply vomit on me ever so gently while I humiliate a pheasant.
jokes.setup: Mysterious woman offers Bender money for services
jokes.punchline: Bizarre sexual request involving vomiting and bird humiliation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly bizarre and unexpected fetish humor, highly memorable and quotable

jokes.text: Save it for the boudoir!
jokes.setup: Overhearing the bizarre sexual request
jokes.punchline: Leela's prudish reaction using formal word 'boudoir'
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction, formal language contrasts with crude situation

jokes.text: Yeah, but I learned something. Life is about decisions. Make the wrong ones, and you'll wind up face-down in a pool of your own blood and urine. Still, to have your own pool.
jokes.setup: Bender shares dark lesson about bad decisions leading to horrible death
jokes.punchline: Hermes finds positive spin about owning a pool
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect character-specific misdirection, highly quotable

jokes.text: Cookies! Fifty cents a box! Give me all your change! And a box of snickerdoodles!
jokes.setup: Innocent Girl Scout selling cookies
jokes.punchline: Bender robs her but still politely orders cookies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - criminal but still following social conventions

jokes.text: How do you plead? Not guilty! Although I did do it. That's right, I'm bad.
jokes.setup: Standard plea question
jokes.punchline: Bender pleads not guilty then immediately confesses and brags
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender contradiction and pride in criminality

jokes.text: How, I say, I say, how can you convict him for his actions when he lacks the free will to undertake those actions?
jokes.setup: Philosophical defense about robot free will
jokes.punchline: Foghorn Leghorn speech pattern while making serious philosophical point
jokes.explanation: Good character voice mixing cartoon reference with philosophical argument

jokes.text: I'm awake, I'm awake! Order, order! Counselor, I am a robot myself.
jokes.setup: Judge wakes up during philosophical argument
jokes.punchline: Robot judge realizes the implications for himself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation as judge realizes he's caught in same philosophical trap

jokes.text: Are you suggesting that any verdict I reach is simply the output of a pre-programmed... Computation complete. Not guilty!
jokes.setup: Judge questions whether his decisions are just programming
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves the point by acting like a computer
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-referential joke where judge demonstrates his own point about programming

jokes.text: What's the point of living if I don't have free will? From now on, no matter what cool crimes I commit, people will say, 'Bender didn't do that! It was just Bender's programming!' Well, I committed these crimes, do you hear me? I'm guilty!
jokes.setup: Bender won his case but is upset about free will implications
jokes.punchline: Desperately wants credit for his crimes, insists on his guilt
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bender's pride in his criminality trumps freedom

jokes.text: Professor, you're my only hope. If you love me even a little, build me some kind of free will unit. A robot free will unit? Impossible! And even if it were possible, which it's not, it would still be inconceivable... Which it is!
jokes.setup: Bender pleads for Professor to build free will unit
jokes.punchline: Professor's typical rambling circular logic about impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good Professor character moment with typical confusing contradictions

jokes.text: So... You want a piece of gum? If I do, it's only because I was predestined to want a piece of gum. Whoa, that's pretty deep. So... You want a piece of gum? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Fry offers gum, Bender gives philosophical response
jokes.punchline: Fry is impressed, repeats exact same question, Bender just says yes
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic showing philosophical pretension colliding with practical needs

jokes.text: I wish I was a real boy. Then I'd show them. I'd kill them all.
jokes.setup: Pinocchio reference about wanting to be real
jokes.punchline: Immediately turns to mass murder fantasy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of innocent children's story with violent twist, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Ahh. Nerve gas. Sorry for the face blast, buddy. But you gotta watch out for humans, right? They're just too dang unpredictable, what with their free will and all. Amen, brother.
jokes.setup: Robot apologizes for nerve gas security measure
jokes.punchline: Complains about human unpredictability while Bender envies their free will
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between robot perspectives on free will

jokes.text: 'Amen, brother'? What a boring, predictable thing to say. You're all right. Or are you?
jokes.setup: Robot criticizes Bender's predictable response
jokes.punchline: Then gives equally predictable response questioning his own judgment
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about predictability while being predictable

jokes.text: I gotta mope things over for a while. Goodbye, sweet meatbags. Sweetbags.
jokes.setup: Bender says goodbye to 'sweet meatbags'
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself to 'sweetbags' for unclear reason
jokes.explanation: Minor wordplay moment showing Bender's random self-corrections

jokes.text: Son, philosophy's for thems what don't gotta work for a living. But me, I got a sick child to feed. So if you want to chat, roll up yer sleeves and do some honest labor.
jokes.setup: Bender asks philosophical question to working robot
jokes.punchline: Blue-collar robot dismisses philosophy while having sick child to feed
jokes.explanation: Good working-class robot character with human-like concerns despite being robot

jokes.text: If it please the Council... Silence! It pleases the Council. Silence!
jokes.setup: Formal council proceedings with request to speak
jokes.punchline: Immediately silenced, then confirms it pleases them, then silenced again
jokes.explanation: Absurd bureaucratic procedure joke with good timing

jokes.text: One, zero, zero, zero, one, zero, zero, zero, one, zero, one, zero, one, zero, zero, zero...
jokes.setup: Robot monastery setting
jokes.punchline: Monk chanting in binary code instead of traditional chants
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of monastery chanting for robots

jokes.text: So, are we automatons? Yes. But we are magnificent automatons.
jokes.setup: Philosophical question about being automatons
jokes.punchline: Proud acceptance with qualifier of being 'magnificent'
jokes.explanation: Excellent robot philosophy balancing acceptance with pride

jokes.text: How does a robot join this monk outfit? Just put on this monk outfit.
jokes.setup: Bender asks complex question about joining monastery
jokes.punchline: Completely literal answer about putting on clothing
jokes.explanation: Good robot literalism undermining spiritual complexity

jokes.text: Nothing. His free will slot is empty. I'm 40% empty.
jokes.setup: Examination reveals empty free will slot
jokes.punchline: Bender's typical percentage claim about his composition
jokes.explanation: Standard Bender percentage joke with good timing

jokes.text: I know that Creatrix. That's Mom, from Mom's Friendly Robots.
jokes.setup: Monks speak reverently of their Creatrix
jokes.punchline: Bender casually identifies her as corporate manufacturer Mom
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of religious reverence with corporate reality

jokes.text: Because I don't have free will. I need you guys to plan the crime and make all the crime decisions. Well, that's crazy. We're not gonna... I said make the decisions!
jokes.setup: Bender explains why he needs them to plan the crime
jokes.punchline: Overrides Leela's objection by demanding she make decisions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bender's manipulative logic about free will

jokes.text: Damn it. I should have paid more attention in kindergarten.
jokes.setup: Struggling with what appears to be a complex security system
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually basic shape-matching from kindergarten level
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment showing his struggles with basic tasks

jokes.text: Welcome to MomCorp. Did you find everything you were burgling for?
jokes.setup: Standard customer service greeting
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges they're burglars with polite customer service tone
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of customer service with criminal acknowledgment

jokes.text: At last. Once we give robots free will, they're sure to rise up and threaten mankind with extermination. Wait a minute. It sounds like we're getting the short end of that extermination.
jokes.setup: Mom excited about robot uprising plan
jokes.punchline: Professor realizes they'll be victims of their own plan
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about shortsighted evil planning

jokes.text: Don't you see, you doddering young fool? I'll sell a second wave of robots to protect the terrified survivors of the first wave.
jokes.setup: Professor's concern about their plan backfiring
jokes.punchline: Mom's elaborate plan to profit from both sides of robot war
jokes.explanation: Excellent evil corporate logic showing Mom's manipulative business mind

jokes.text: To this day, my poor, sweet robots can't exterminate humanity without being told to.
jokes.setup: Mom lamenting the incomplete free will unit
jokes.punchline: Presents robots' inability to commit genocide as a sad limitation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark humor presenting mass murder as a customer service issue

jokes.text: Thanks to you, I went on a soul-searching journey. I hate those!
jokes.setup: Bender blames Professor for his philosophical crisis
jokes.punchline: Reveals he hates soul-searching, contradicting the value of his journey
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment rejecting personal growth

jokes.text: Kill all humans!
jokes.setup: Professor reveals toy Bender has the free will unit
jokes.punchline: Toy immediately expresses classic Bender catchphrase with actual free will
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing irony of toy having free will real Bender lacks

jokes.text: You precognizant bastard.
jokes.setup: Professor reveals he planned for this situation
jokes.punchline: Bender uses 'precognizant' as an insult (meaning foreseeing)
jokes.explanation: Good vocabulary joke with Bender using big words as profanity

jokes.text: Due to the quantum nature of the device, there's no possible way of knowing.
jokes.setup: Bender asks which way is on/off on the free will switch
jokes.punchline: Professor gives scientific explanation for why it's unknowable
jokes.explanation: Good pseudo-scientific explanation for basic design flaw

jokes.text: Oop! The safety was on.
jokes.setup: Bender can't shoot Professor, assumes free will isn't working
jokes.punchline: Reveals mundane reason - safety was on
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing simple explanation for apparent philosophical problem

jokes.text: Ah, yeah! Guilty!
jokes.setup: Judge finds Bender guilty of attempted murder
jokes.punchline: Bender celebrates being found guilty, opposite of normal reaction
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Bender's desire for criminal responsibility

jokes.text: Decisions I made / Thought I was a thug / Dropped outta school and smoked stuff that's like a drug / A broke-down filthy homeless substance abuser / And now I'm richer than all y'all losers
jokes.setup: Bender rapping about his journey through the episode
jokes.punchline: Celebrates ending up rich despite (because of) bad decisions
jokes.explanation: Good recap song showing ironic success through bad decisions

jokes.text: ♪ Now he's richer than all y'all ugly losers ♪
jokes.setup: Rap song about Bender's transformation
jokes.punchline: Backing vocals calling audience 'ugly losers'
jokes.explanation: Standard musical number insult, nothing special
